title: Case/Care Management * Adults With Disabilities/Health Conditions - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Case/Care Management * Adults With Disabilities/Health Conditions 58 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - Adults With Disabilities/Health Conditions Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions Chronic/Severe Mental Illness Deafness Disabilities Issues Hard of Hearing Older Adults Hispanic/Latino Community Immigrants Refugees/Entrants/Asylees Homeless Veterans Veterans Native American Community AIDS/HIV Issues ARC UMATILLA COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides connections to resources such as case management services, assistance with advocacy, disability awareness training, education, recreational programs, and vocational training. 215 W Orchard Avenue Hermiston, OR 978381738 Eligibility: Individuals who have intellectual and developmental disabilities and their families Hours: Monday 9am-noon Email (541) 567-7615 Get Directions Visit Website More Details AVANTI ELDERCARE RESOURCES INC Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides consultations and assessments for care and housing needs, evaluates financial and insurance resources, household and care management, monitoring/oversight, money management, and guardian and conservator services. Facilitates communication with health care providers, provides patient accompaniment to medical appointments, and home visits available for assessments and bill paying. "Just in Case" plan and estate-planning services also available. 911 Country Club Road Suite 250 Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Adults age 18 and older with cognitive impairments, mental health challenges, brain injury, persons with complex health conditions, those who have been subject to financial or emotional abuse of an adult or elder, and persons who need help managing financial or health-care matters. Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-4:30pm or by appointment On call 24 hours per day / 7 days per week for urgent needs of established clients Email (541) 344-7712 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS COUNTY SOCIAL SERVICES OREGON PROJECT INDEPENDENCE–MEDICAID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides in-home care. Services include housekeeping, personal care, laundry, shopping, transportation, and meal preparation and delivery. Adult day services (non-residential community-based programs, providing services for part of the day). Registered nurse services (one-time or intermittent assessment, service planning, provider training, and education). Also offers case management and options counseling (counseling to support informed decision-making on services based on strengths, needs, and preferences). Service hours per person vary depending on assessed need and funding amounts. In-home service hours are available up to 20 hours per week, based on individual need. Benefits will not be subject to estate recovery. 2051 Kaen Road #135 Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Adults, age 18 and older, who meet the following criteria: -Need help from another person with personal care tasks -Income no greater than 400% of the Federal Poverty Level -Assets no greater than the average cost of six months in a nursing facility -Currently living in their own home and not a provider-operator or owned facility or foster/care home Hours: Monday-Thursday 7am-6pm (503) 655-8640 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ACTION PROGRAM OF EAST CENTRAL OREGON HERMISTON OREGON PROJECT INDEPENDENCE – MEDICAID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides in-home care. Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOUTH COAST BUSINESS MAIN OFFICE OREGON PROJECT INDEPENDENCE – MEDICAID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides in-home care. Services include housekeeping, personal care, laundry, shopping, transportation, and meal preparation and delivery. Adult day services (non-residential community-based programs, providing services for part of the day). Registered nurse services (one-time or intermittent assessment, service planning, medication monitoring, provider training, and education). Also offers case management and options counseling (counseling to support informed decision-making on services based on strengths, needs, and preferences). Service hours per person vary depending on assessed need and funding amounts. Hours may range from 5-20 hours a month. Benefits will not be subject to estate recovery. 800 N Bayshore Drive Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Adults age 18 and older who meet the following criteria: -Need help from another person with personal care tasks -Income no greater than 400% of the Federal Poverty Level -Assets no greater than the average cost of six months in a nursing facility -Currently living in their own home and not a provider-operator or owned facility or foster/care home Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 269-2013 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOUTH COAST BUSINESS SENIOR SERVICES OREGON PROJECT INDEPENDENCE – MEDICAID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides in-home care. Services include housekeeping, personal care, laundry, shopping, transportation, and meal preparation and delivery. Adult day services (non-residential community-based programs, providing services for part of the day). Registered nurse services (one-time or intermittent assessment, service planning, medication monitoring, provider training, and education). Also offers case management and options counseling (counseling to support informed decision-making on services based on strengths, needs, and preferences). Service hours per person vary depending on assessed need and funding amounts. Hours may range from 5-20 hours a month. Benefits will not be subject to estate recovery. 1216 Moore Street Suite #214 Brookings, OR 97415 Eligibility: Adults age 18 and older who meet the following criteria: -Need help from another person with personal care tasks -Income no greater than 400% of the Federal Poverty Level -Assets no greater than the average cost of six months in a nursing facility -Currently living in their own home and not a provider-operator or owned facility or foster/care home Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 412-8820 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRANSITIONS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides non-clinical case management services that focus on identifying areas requiring support, and helping individuals navigate and access available community resources. 2611 NE Courtney Drive Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Individuals with a serious health condition and a limited life expectancy Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 322-1289 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UMATILLA COUNTY DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES UMATILLA COUNTY DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a wide range of services that may include networking and outreach, facilitation of Individual Support Plan (ISP) meetings, assisting in the referral process to community service agencies for supports, providing support to individuals and families in a variety of planning meetings, assisting in finding residential placement and employment services, as well as referrals to support services brokerages. The DD program staff may also review complaints of abuse and immediately take steps to assess further risk. In some cases, protective services may be offered to avoid further harm. 817 SE 13th Street Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Must have the all of the following: -A disability that originated in the brain -The disability must be established prior the age of 22, or in the case of intellectual disability the condition must be determined before 18 years of age. -Must result in significant impairments in at least two of the following areas of daily function: Self-care, communication, cognition, mobility, self direction, capacity of independent living, and/or economic self sufficiency -The impairments must not be primarily related to: substance abuse, mental illness, an emotional disorder, ADHD, a learning disability, or sensory impairment. Impairments must be expected to last indefinitely. Hours: Monday-Thursday 7am-6pm, Friday 8am-noon Email (541) 278-4450 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WASHINGTON COUNTY DISABILITY, AGING AND VETERAN SERVICES OREGON PROJECT INDEPENDENCE – MEDICAID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides in-home care. Services include housekeeping, personal care, laundry, shopping, transportation, and meal preparation and delivery. Adult day services (non-residential community-based programs, providing services for part of the day). Registered nurse services (one-time or intermittent assessment, service planning, medication monitoring, provider training, and education). Also offers case management and options counseling (counseling to support informed decision-making on services based on strengths, needs, and preferences). Service hours per person vary depending on assessed need and funding amounts. Participants may receive up to 20 hours of caregiving assistance per week. Benefits will not be subject to estate recovery. 5240 NE Elam Young Parkway Suite 300 Hillsboro, OR 97124 Eligibility: Adults, age 18 and older, who meet the following criteria: -Need help from another person with personal care tasks -Income no greater than 400% of the federal poverty Level -Assets no greater than the average cost of six months in a nursing facility -Currently living in
